  "judgement": "FP",
  "analysis": "The report is a false positive. The local stack buffer `buf` is created only in the wraparound branch of `chcr_write_cpl_set_tcb_ulp()` and that branch exits immediately via `return chcr_copy_to_txd(...)`, so the reported sink `return pos` at line 825 is not reachable on any path where `buf` exists. Separately, the only value that escapes from the `buf` branch is not `buf` itself: `__chcr_write_cpl_set_tcb_ulp(..., buf, ...)` merely formats a temporary CPL into the stack buffer, its return value is ignored, and `chcr_copy_to_txd()` copies the bytes into the NIC TX descriptor ring and returns a cursor within that ring. As a result, no pointer to stack storage is returned to callers, and no CWE-416/use-after-return condition is reachable in real execution.",
  "FP_Reason": ["D3"],
  "evidence": "`drivers/net/ethernet/chelsio/inline_crypto/ch_ktls/chcr_ktls.c:804-814` enters the only branch where `buf` exists: `u8 buf[48] = {0}; __chcr_write_cpl_set_tcb_ulp(..., buf, ...); return chcr_copy_to_txd(buf, &q->q, pos, CHCR_SET_TCB_FIELD_LEN);`. This branch returns before line 825, so `return pos` at `chcr_ktls.c:825` cannot execute with `buf` live. `drivers/net/ethernet/chelsio/inline_crypto/ch_ktls/chcr_common.h:58-79` shows `chcr_copy_to_txd()` does `memcpy(pos, src, length)` or wraps with `memcpy(q->desc, src + left, length - left)`, then returns `p` / `p + 1` derived from `pos` or `q->desc`, never from `src`. `drivers/net/ethernet/chelsio/cxgb4/cxgb4.h:852-854` defines `q->desc` as the HW Tx descriptor ring and `q->stat` as the queue status entry, confirming the returned pointer is ring memory. Also, `drivers/net/ethernet/chelsio/inline_crypto/ch_ktls/chcr_ktls.c:865-876` shows callers seed `pos` from `&q->q.desc[q->q.pidx]` and use the return only as the next descriptor-write cursor.",
